//: Realtime Database security rules for Card Night online multiplayer.
//readme: Firebase only stores rooms/* (lobbies + in-game message relay). Coins, profile, stats and achievements are LOCAL to each device (AsyncStorage) and never touch Firebase. Deploy via Firebase console -> Realtime Database -> Rules (paste + Publish) or `firebase deploy --only database`. Auth is anonymous (see game/firebase.js), so `auth != null` mainly ties every write to a stable uid, which is what powers the host/player ownership checks below.

$code.//: Any signed-in device may READ a room: joiners must read it to check status before joining, and clients read net/* to receive game state. The 4-char room code is the shared secret. No personal data lives here beyond chosen display names.
$code.//write: Create: the writer must make themselves the host. After that: only the host may write room-level fields (settings, status, teardown). Player-slot and net writes are granted by the deeper rules below (rules only ADD access down the tree).
$code..write: auth != null && ( (!data.exists() && newData.child('host').val() === auth.uid) || (data.exists() && !newData.exists() && data.child('host').val() === auth.uid) || (data.exists() && newData.exists() && data.child('host').val() === auth.uid && newData.child('host').val() === auth.uid) )

$uid.//write: A player may create/remove ONLY their own slot (join/leave/onDisconnect). The host may also remove any slot (handled via the room-level host write). No one can write someone else's slot.

broadcast.//write: Host -> everyone. Only the host may write the broadcast channel.

private.//write: Host -> one client. Only the host may write private channels.

toHost.//write: Client -> host queue. Only a device that is actually a player in this room may push. The host drains (deletes) entries via the room-level host write.
